text: The more you praise and celebrate your life, the more there is in life to celebrate.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: Count your age by friends, not years. Count your life by smiles, not tears.
author: John Lennon

text: Life is a party. Dress for it.
author: Audrey Hepburn

text: Celebrate what you want to see more of.
author: Tom Peters

text: Today you are You, that is truer than true. There is no one alive who is Youer than You.
author: Dr. Seuss

text: Growing old is mandatory, but growing up is optional.
author: Walt Disney

text: A birthday is just the first day of another 365-day journey around the sun. Enjoy the trip.
author: Unknown

text: Age is merely the number of years the world has been enjoying you.
author: Unknown

text: The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
author: Walt Disney

text: Cheers to a new year and another chance for us to get it right.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: You are never too old to set another goal or to dream a new dream.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Let us celebrate the occasion with wine and sweet words.
author: Plautus

text: Age is an issue of mind over matter. If you don't mind, it doesn't matter.
author: Mark Twain

text: Wrinkles should merely indicate where smiles have been.
author: Mark Twain

text: The best way to remember your wife's birthday is to forget it once.
author: E. Joseph Cossman

text: Because time itself is like a spiral, something special happens on your birthday each year: the same energy that God invested in you at birth is present once again.
author: Menachem Mendel Schneerson

text: Every year on your birthday, you get a chance to start new.
author: Sammy Hagar

text: Birthdays are nature's way of telling us to eat more cake.
author: Unknown

text: It is not how old you are, but how you are old.
author: Jules Renard

text: May you live all the days of your life.
author: Jonathan Swift

text: Don't just count your years, make your years count.
author: George Meredith

text: The secret of staying young is to live honestly, eat slowly, and lie about your age.
author: Lucille Ball

text: There is still no cure for the common birthday.
author: John Glenn

text: Youth is the gift of nature, but age is a work of art.
author: Stanislaw Jerzy Lec

text: Old age is like everything else. To make a success of it, you've got to start young.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: The more you celebrate your life, the more there is in life to celebrate.
author: Unknown

text: Anyone who stops learning is old, whether at twenty or eighty. Anyone who keeps learning stays young.
author: Henry Ford

text: All the world is birthday cake, so take a piece, but not too much.
author: George Harrison

text: Success is not the key to happiness. Happiness is the key to success. If you love what you are doing, you will be successful.
author: Albert Schweitzer

text: A milestone is less about the destination and more about the journey that brought you here.
author: Unknown

text: Celebrate the small wins; they add up to big victories.
author: Unknown

text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
author: Dalai Lama

text: The purpose of our lives is to be happy.
author: Dalai Lama

text: Joy is the simplest form of gratitude.
author: Karl Barth

text: Find ecstasy in life; the mere sense of living is joy enough.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: Enjoy the little things, for one day you may look back and realize they were the big things.
author: Robert Brault

text: Life is short, and it is up to you to make it sweet.
author: Sarah Louise Delany

text: Shout for joy, all you who are upright in heart!
author: Unknown

text: Celebrate your success and stand strong when adversity hits, for when the storm clouds come in, the eagles soar while the small birds take cover.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: To succeed in life, you need three things: a wishbone, a backbone, and a funny bone.
author: Reba McEntire

text: Dance like nobody's watching. Love like you've never been hurt. Sing like nobody's listening. Live like it's heaven on earth.
author: Unknown

text: Write it on your heart that every day is the best day in the year.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: This is the day which the Lord hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
author: Unknown

text: Nothing is worth more than this day.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: Every day is a good day. There is something to learn, care and celebrate.
author: Amit Ray

text: The manner in which we celebrate reveals what we truly value.
author: Unknown

text: A party without cake is just a meeting.
author: Julia Child

text: Life should not only be lived, it should be celebrated.
author: Osho

text: Celebration is a kind of food we all need in our lives, and each individual brings a special recipe or offering, so that together we will make a great feast.
author: Corita Kent

text: Our happiest moments as tourists always seem to come when we stumble upon one thing while in pursuit of something else.
author: Lawrence Block

text: The trick in life is learning how to deal with it.
author: Helen Mirren

text: Where there is love there is life.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Congratulations are in order, but so is a reminder: the best is yet to come.
author: Unknown

text: Graduation is not the end; it's the beginning.
author: Orrin Hatch

text: Your education is a dress rehearsal for a life that is yours to lead.
author: Nora Ephron

text: Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you have imagined.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: Wherever you go, go with all your heart.
author: Confucius

text: And in the end, it's not the years in your life that count. It's the life in your years.
author: Unknown

text: Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things you didn't do than by the ones you did do.
author: Unknown

text: May your walls know joy, may every room hold laughter, and every window open to great possibility.
author: Mary Anne Radmacher

text: A wedding anniversary is the celebration of love, trust, partnership, tolerance and tenacity. The order varies for any given year.
author: Paul Sweeney

text: A successful marriage requires falling in love many times, always with the same person.
author: Mignon McLaughlin

text: Grow old along with me! The best is yet to be.
author: Robert Browning

text: Whatever our souls are made of, his and mine are the same.
author: Emily Bronte

text: A baby is God's opinion that the world should go on.
author: Carl Sandburg

text: Babies are bits of stardust, blown from the hand of God.
author: Larry Barretto

text: A new baby is like the beginning of all things: wonder, hope, a dream of possibilities.
author: Eda LeShan

text: Retirement is not the end of the road. It is the beginning of the open highway.
author: Unknown

text: Retire from work, but not from life.
author: M.K. Soni

text: Don't cry because it's over, smile because it happened.
author: Dr. Seuss

text: How lucky I am to have something that makes saying goodbye so hard.
author: A.A. Milne

text: Every new beginning comes from some other beginning's end.
author: Seneca

text: Cheers to the nights we will never forget and the friends we will never remember them without.
author: Unknown

text: Good friends, good books, and a sleepy conscience: this is the ideal life.
author: Mark Twain

text: There is nothing better than a friend, unless it is a friend with chocolate.
author: Linda Grayson

text: Laughter is timeless, imagination has no age, and dreams are forever.
author: Walt Disney

text: We do not remember days, we remember moments.
author: Cesare Pavese

text: Life is what happens to you while you're busy making other plans.
author: John Lennon

text: The best is yet to come.
author: Frank Sinatra

text: Sometimes you will never know the value of a moment until it becomes a memory.
author: Dr. Seuss

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Success is a journey, not a destination. The doing is often more important than the outcome.
author: Arthur Ashe

text: The harder the battle, the sweeter the victory.
author: Les Brown

text: Victory is sweetest when you've known defeat.
author: Malcolm Forbes

text: Great things never came from comfort zones.
author: Unknown

text: The tassel was worth the hassle.
author: Unknown

text: Champagne is one of the elegant extras in life.
author: Charles Dickens

text: In victory, you deserve Champagne. In defeat you need it.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

text: Come quickly, I am tasting the stars!
author: Dom Perignon

text: One cannot think well, love well, sleep well, if one has not dined well.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: Happiness is only real when shared.
author: Christopher McCandless

text: Rejoice with your family in the beautiful land of life.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The most I can do for my friend is simply be his friend.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: Each day comes bearing its own gifts. Untie the ribbons.
author: Ruth Ann Schabacker

text: Today is your day! Your mountain is waiting, so get on your way!
author: Dr. Seuss

text: Life is either a daring adventure or nothing at all.
author: Helen Keller

text: May the best of your todays be the worst of your tomorrows.
author: Unknown

text: Piglet noticed that even though he had a Very Small Heart, it could hold a rather large amount of Gratitude.
author: A.A. Milne

text: Yesterday is history, tomorrow is a mystery, today is a gift of God, which is why we call it the present.
author: Unknown
